mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rate above 4.5% signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates applied to equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.59% as of 2006-03-01 indicates active tightening cycle
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices recession risk.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 50,000 persons in single month
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite payroll declines, or correlation between PAYEMS changes and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in housing starts below 1,800 thousand units signals weakening residential investment demand and forward-looking consumer confidence deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as construction and related sectors contract.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2,120 thousand units as of 2006-02-01 indicates strong housing demand
regime_hint: housing cycle peak with subsequent demand deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 65% of instances when HOUST < 1800, or Spearman rank correlation between HOUST and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth above 2% month-over-month signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and pricing power, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors recognize sustained profit margin expansion potential.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 111.21 index points as of 2006-03-01
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when INDPRO growth is positive, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in retail sales growth and housing starts signals synchronized demand weakness across consumer and residential sectors, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices aggregate demand expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth < 0.5% AND HOUST month-over-month decline > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 361,046 million dollars and HOUST at 2,120 thousand units as of 2006-02-01
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (joint signal) produces lower predictive power than either signal alone (Rank IC difference < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate combined with declining payroll employment signals accelerating labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings revisions turn negative.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points in 3 months AND PAYEMS declines > 30,000 persons
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7% and PAYEMS at 134,966 thousand as of 2006-03-01
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI growth above 2.5% annually combined with elevated federal funds rate above 4.5% signals restrictive real monetary policy that constrains equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices near-term rate expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 199.1 index and FEDFUNDS at 4.59% as of 2006-03-01
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect does not improve predictive power over single-series models (Rank IC difference < 0.005).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured quarterly below 2% signals economic deceleration that reduces corporate earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice growth multiples downward in anticipation of profit margin compression.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11,248.3 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2005-10-01
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 65% of instances when GDPC1 growth < 2%, or Spearman rank correlation between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).
